objective.name: C2-150 Aegis lane-health report
objective.outcome: Implement a v1 read-only CLI/report that computes the Aegis lane-health metrics from existing Multica data fields and redacted ForgePilot trace summaries.
deliverables: Read-only lane-health report command over existing database fields with optional daemon /health snapshot input
Verdict model that separates v1 HEALTHY/UNHEALTHY drivers from context-only metrics
Redacted JSON output limited to IDs, enums, counts, timestamps, and durations
Focused tests proving C2-123-style UNHEALTHY and C2-142-style HEALTHY sample behavior

may_build: Standalone read-only backend report command under server/cmd
Small internal package for metric and verdict calculation
Direct SELECT queries against existing issue, agent_task_queue, agent_runtime, agent, activity_log, and issue metadata fields
Optional daemon /health JSON snapshot parsing with graceful unavailable state
Trace parsing limited to check names and PASS/FAIL-like status enums
must_not_build: Raw issue titles, descriptions, comment bodies, prompts, secrets, credentials, PHI, raw FLT, raw logs, or free-text trace failure reasons in output

success_checks: cd server && env GOMAXPROCS=2 GOCACHE=/tmp/c2-150-go-build-cache GOMODCACHE=/tmp/c2-150-go-mod-cache go test -p=1 ./internal/lanehealth ./cmd/lane_health_report
notes: Active v0.2 validation is attempted against the companion YAML contract, but the current Multica repo does not contain contracts/active/v0.2/schemas/agent-contract.schema.json.
The command is intentionally direct-report tooling, not an API feature.
Daemon active_task_count is live-only; when absent, output must mark daemon health unavailable and rely on persisted queue/runtime data.
